title: TodoWrite — Structured Task Planning
description: The agent uses a TodoManager to plan work before executing it. Todos create a structured checklist that keeps the agent on track across long tasks. A nag reminder fires if the agent forgets to update its todos.

steps.content: Build a REST API for a bookstore with GET /books and POST /books endpoints using Spring Boot.
steps.annotation: A multi-step task. Without a todo list the agent might lose track of steps. The TodoManager enforces discipline.

steps.content:
[
  { "id": "1", "task": "Create Spring Boot project structure with pom.xml", "status": "in_progress" },
  { "id": "2", "task": "Create Book entity class", "status": "pending" },
  { "id": "3", "task": "Create BookController with GET /books", "status": "pending" },
  { "id": "4", "task": "Add POST /books endpoint", "status": "pending" },
  { "id": "5", "task": "Compile and verify", "status": "pending" }
]
steps.annotation: The agent writes its plan as structured todos BEFORE doing any work. Each todo has an id, task description, and status. This is the 'plan' phase.

steps.content: Todos saved (5 items). Current: #1 — Create Spring Boot project structure with pom.xml
steps.annotation: TodoManager acknowledges the plan. Item #1 is marked in_progress, giving the agent a clear next action.
Executing todo #1: creating the Maven project file. The agent works through its checklist item by item.
pom.xml created. The agent should now update todo #1 to 'completed' and move to #2.

steps.content:
TodoManager: You have mass todos. You should update them as you work through your tasks. Current todos:
#1 Create Spring Boot project structure with pom.xml [in_progress]
#2 Create Book entity class [pending]
#3 Create BookController with GET /books [pending]
#4 Add POST /books endpoint [pending]
#5 Compile and verify [pending]
steps.annotation: NAG REMINDER: The system injects this reminder because the agent completed work but forgot to update its todos. This is appended to the system prompt to keep the agent honest.

steps.content:
[
  { "id": "1", "task": "Create Spring Boot project structure with pom.xml", "status": "completed" },
  { "id": "2", "task": "Create Book entity class", "status": "in_progress" },
  { "id": "3", "task": "Create BookController with GET /books", "status": "pending" },
  { "id": "4", "task": "Add POST /books endpoint", "status": "pending" },
  { "id": "5", "task": "Compile and verify", "status": "pending" }
]
steps.annotation: The agent responds to the nag by updating its todos. #1 is now completed, #2 is in_progress. The TodoManager ensures nothing falls through the cracks.

steps.content: Todos updated. Completed: 1/5. Current: #2 — Create Book entity class
steps.annotation: Progress is tracked. The agent continues executing its plan methodically.
Executing todo #2: creating the Book entity. The structured plan keeps the agent focused on one task at a time.
Book entity created. The TodoManager pattern — plan first, execute step by step, update progress — prevents the agent from going off-track on complex tasks.
